rationale: Both candidates independently reconstruct the one substantive ground-truth concern (already_submitted removal is safe because both submit_initial/submit_update reject list prompts for diffusion), so recall is comparable. X's findings are tighter and all grounded — the multi-element-list note is correctly framed as intentional/non-blocking and the regression-test ask is concise. Y pads with a redundant 'please confirm' on something it already claims to have verified (ZImagePipeline's DiffusionOutput return type) and raises a scope-creep 'major' finding about pre-existing, untouched code (orchestrator.py:1264 multi-element handling), which weakens precision. Y's overly cautious 'FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED' verdict also diverges from the actual outcome (clean LGTM/LGTM approval).
